title: Stay-at-home requirements during the COVID-19 pandemic
subtitle: If policies vary at the subnational level, the index is shown as the response level of the strictest sub-region.
caption:
Stay-at-home requirements during the COVID-19 pandemic

If policies vary at the subnational level, the index is shown as the response level of the strictest sub-region.

This choropleth world map displays stay-at-home requirements as of December 31, 2022, using a color-coded legend that ranges from "No measures" (dark blue) to "Required (few exceptions)" (red), with intermediate categories for "Recommended" (light blue) and "Required (except essentials)" (orange). The vast majority of the world's landmass, including all of South America, Africa, Europe, Russia, Canada, and Australia, is shaded in dark blue, indicating that no stay-at-home measures were in place. A distinct cluster in South Asia breaks this pattern: India, Nepal, and Bangladesh appear in orange, signifying that stay-at-home orders were required except for essential activities. China and the United States are shaded in light blue, placing them in the "Recommended" category, while Greenland is marked with diagonal hatching to denote no data. The visual distribution shows that by the end of 2022, strict stay-at-home mandates had been lifted in most regions, with the primary exceptions concentrated in South Asia and specific policy recommendations remaining in North America and East Asia.
